health_notes: Demo: pregnant mother, diabetic father, one child, low salt diet, soda should be corrected from 3 to 1.

analysis.summary: My Household is set up as a mixed household with 4 member(s), a moderate activity level, and a balanced nutrition goal.

focus.Sugar: Prioritize low added-sugar foods and pair carbohydrates with fiber or protein.
focus.Sodium: Watch packaged sauces, instant foods, processed meats, and salty snacks.
focus.Saturated fat: Limit fried foods, full-fat dairy, fatty meats, and rich bakery items.
focus.Father sugar risk: This member has diabetes context, so sweet drinks and high-sugar snacks should be reduced first.
focus.Father sodium risk: This member has blood-pressure context, so salty packaged foods need stronger monitoring.
focus.Child nutrition: Child member profile increases attention on sugar, calcium, protein, and fiber quality.

recommendations: Keep sweet drinks, desserts, and highly refined snacks as occasional purchases.
Choose lower-sodium alternatives and add more fresh fruit, vegetables, and legumes.
Shift repeated purchases toward oats, beans, nuts, fish, and unsaturated cooking oils.
